d: Frame is the primary container in Figma. Use frames for artboards, screens, layout containers, and component wrappers. Frames clip content by default (toggle via 'Clip content' checkbox in design panel). Frames support: auto layout, constraints, layout grids, fills, strokes, effects, corner radius, and blend modes. Frames can be nested infinitely. Create with F key or drag from toolbar.

Frame width and height each independently support three sizing modes: Fixed (explicit pixel value), Hug contents (shrinks to fit children, only available with auto layout), Fill container (expands to fill parent, only available inside auto layout parent). Set sizing in the design panel W/H dropdowns. Fixed sizes can use any positive number. Hug and Fill are context-dependent on auto layout being present.

Top-level frames sitting directly on the canvas act as artboards. They appear in the prototype presentation, are listed in the left panel pages, show up in exports, and define the viewport for prototype flows. Nested frames do not act as artboards. Top-level frame names appear as page titles in prototype view. Set device presets (iPhone 16: 393x852, Desktop: 1440x900, etc.) from the frame preset dropdown.

Frames clip overflowing children by default. Toggle 'Clip content' in the design panel to show or hide overflow. When clip is off, children extending beyond the frame boundary remain visible on canvas but are still logically inside the frame. Clip content is essential for scroll regions in prototypes: enable clip + set overflow scrolling (horizontal, vertical, or both) to create scrollable areas.

Groups are lightweight wrappers with NO layout intelligence. Groups do NOT support: auto layout, constraints, layout grids, independent fills, independent strokes, or corner radius. Groups exist solely to bundle layers together. Groups resize proportionally: when you resize a group, ALL children scale proportionally (width, height, position, font size). Use groups only for temporary organization or moving multiple elements simultaneously.

Use Frame when you need: auto layout, constraints, clipping, fills/strokes, grid layouts, or fixed dimensions independent of children. Use Group only for: temporary multi-select bundling or proportional scaling of children. Convert group to frame: select group, right-click > Frame selection (or Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+G creates group, then can change in design panel). Group shortcut: Ctrl/Cmd+G. Ungroup: Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G. Prefer frames over groups in almost all design work.

Sections are canvas-level organizers that sit above frames in the hierarchy. Sections do NOT affect layout, do not clip content, and cannot have auto layout. Use sections to organize flows, screens, and design states on the canvas. Sections can be named and assigned a fill color for visual distinction. Sections are visible in the layers panel and help with navigation in large files. Create via the Section tool (Shift+S) or toolbar.

Sections are ideal for dev handoff: organize by feature, user flow, or screen state. Name sections descriptively: 'Login Flow', 'Dashboard - States', 'Component Library'. Color-code sections by status: blue for in-progress, green for approved, red for needs revision. Sections can contain multiple top-level frames (artboards). Sections can be collapsed in the layers panel to reduce clutter. Sections do not appear in prototype presentations.

Layer order in the left panel determines z-index: top of the panel = front of the canvas (rendered last, appears on top). Bottom of panel = back of canvas (rendered first, appears behind). Reorder layers by dragging in the panel or using shortcuts: Ctrl/Cmd+] bring forward, Ctrl/Cmd+[ send backward, Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+] bring to front, Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+[ send to back. Layer order matters for boolean operations (front vs back shape).

Name layers with descriptive nouns: Header, CTA Button, Avatar, Sidebar Nav. Use slash hierarchy for component layers: Card/Header/Title. Avoid default names like 'Frame 47' or 'Rectangle 12'. Lock layers to prevent accidental edits: Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+L (or click lock icon). Hide layers: click eye icon or Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+H. Hidden layers are excluded from exports by default. Rename layer: double-click in layers panel or select and press Ctrl/Cmd+R.

Select all: Ctrl/Cmd+A (selects all in current context). Deep select into nested frames: Ctrl/Cmd+click drills into one level at a time, or double-click to enter a frame then click children. Select layer from canvas: click once selects top-level parent, Ctrl/Cmd+click selects the deepest target. Hold Shift to add/remove from selection. Drag-select: draw a rectangle to select all layers within it. Select child without entering frame: Ctrl/Cmd+click directly on the child.

Select all matching layers: use Edit > Select All with Same... to match by fill, stroke, font, instance, effect, or other properties. Smart selection: select multiple similar objects, then use the pink handles to adjust spacing uniformly between all selected objects. Tidy up: select multiple elements and click 'Tidy up' in the alignment toolbar (or Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+T) to auto-arrange into a clean grid. These tools accelerate bulk edits across complex designs.

Alignment options (in toolbar or right-click > Align): Align left, Align horizontal center, Align right, Align top, Align vertical center, Align bottom. When one object is selected, alignment is relative to parent frame. When multiple objects are selected, alignment is relative to the selection bounding box. Distribute: Distribute horizontal spacing (even gaps horizontally), Distribute vertical spacing (even gaps vertically). Distribute spaces objects evenly between the outermost elements.

Constraints control how children reposition/resize when their parent frame is resized. Horizontal constraints: Left (fixed distance from left), Right (fixed from right), Left & Right (stretches with frame width), Center (stays centered), Scale (proportionally scales with frame). Vertical constraints: Top, Bottom, Top & Bottom (stretches with height), Center, Scale. Set in the design panel or right panel under Constraints. Constraints are relative to the immediate parent frame only.

t: Constraint rules and auto layout interaction
d: Use Left & Right + Top & Bottom for elements that must stretch when the frame resizes (e.g., backgrounds, dividers). Use Center for elements that should stay centered regardless of frame size (e.g., modals, logos). Constraints are DISABLED when the parent frame has auto layout enabled; auto layout controls positioning instead. Scale constraint is useful for proportional illustrations or decorative elements. Constraints only work on direct children of frames, not groups.

Boolean operations combine two or more shapes. Union: merges all shapes, keeps combined area. Subtract: removes the front shape(s) from the back shape. Intersect: keeps only the overlapping area of all shapes. Exclude: keeps only the non-overlapping areas (opposite of intersect). Access via toolbar or Ctrl/Cmd+E to flatten. Layer order matters: the back shape is the base for subtract. Boolean groups are non-destructive; double-click to edit individual shapes within the boolean group.

Flatten (Ctrl/Cmd+E): converts a boolean group into a single merged vector path. This is destructive and cannot be un-flattened. Use flatten when: you need a single path for export, or to simplify complex boolean chains. Before flattening, boolean groups remain editable: double-click to select and move individual sub-shapes. Boolean results can be used in further boolean operations (nested booleans). Boolean operations work on any closed vector shapes, rectangles, ellipses, polygons, and stars.

Masks use one layer to clip the visibility of layers above it. To create: select the mask shape and content layers, right-click > Use as Mask (or Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+M). The BOTTOM layer in the selection becomes the mask shape. All layers above the mask (within the same group/frame) are clipped to the mask shape. Alpha masks use opacity or gradients in the mask layer to create soft, feathered edges. Remove mask by selecting the mask layer and toggling off 'Use as mask' in the design panel.

Standard mask: clips to the filled area of the mask shape. Outline mask: clips to the stroke/outline path of the mask shape (enable via the mask layer's 'Outline' option). Alpha mask: uses the opacity values of the mask layer; fully opaque areas show content, transparent areas hide it. Use gradient fills on mask shapes for smooth fade effects. Masks must exist within a group or frame alongside the masked content. Masks cannot span across different groups.

Export formats: PNG (lossless, supports transparency), JPG (lossy, no transparency, smaller files), SVG (vector, scalable, for icons/illustrations), PDF (vector, for print/documents). Add export settings to any layer via the Export section in the right panel. Multiple export settings can be stacked on one layer for batch export. Click the '+' in Export section to add a format. Export the entire page or individual layers. Use File > Export to export all layers with export settings at once.

Export multipliers handle pixel density: @1x (base), @2x (retina), @3x (super retina), @4x (ultra). For iOS: export @1x, @2x, @3x. For Android density buckets: mdpi=1x, hdpi=1.5x, xhdpi=2x, xxhdpi=3x, xxxhdpi=4x. Add suffix in export settings (e.g., suffix '@2x' produces icon-home@2x.png). Layer names become file names. Slice tool (S key) defines custom rectangular export regions independent of layer boundaries. For SVG export: enable 'Include id attribute' for dev use.

Create prototype connections by switching to Prototype tab and dragging from the blue circle on a frame/element to the destination frame. Triggers: On click (tap), On hover (desktop only), On press (mouse down), On drag, While hovering (continuous), Mouse enter, Mouse leave, After delay (timed, specify ms), Key/Gamepad (specific key press). Each layer can have multiple interactions with different triggers. Prototype starting point: click the play icon on a top-level frame to set it as the flow start.

Actions: Navigate to (go to frame), Open overlay (show frame as overlay), Swap overlay (replace current overlay), Close overlay, Back (go to previous frame), Scroll to (scroll to a layer within same frame), Open link (external URL). Transitions: Instant (no animation), Dissolve, Smart animate (morphs matching layers by name), Move in, Move out, Push, Slide in, Slide out. Direction for Move/Push/Slide: left, right, top, bottom. Duration in ms. Smart animate requires matching layer names between frames.

Easing curves: Linear (constant speed), Ease in (slow start), Ease out (slow end), Ease in and out (slow start and end), Spring (physics-based bounce), Custom bezier (define control points). Overflow scrolling: set on a frame via Prototype tab; options are Horizontal, Vertical, or Both. Frame must have Clip content enabled for scrolling to work. Fixed elements: in Prototype tab, check 'Fix position when scrolling' to pin elements like navbars or FABs during scroll. Fixed elements stay on screen as user scrolls.

Dev Mode (toggle via </> button or Shift+D) shows generated code: CSS (default), iOS (SwiftUI, UIKit), Android (Jetpack Compose, XML). Inspect panel displays: dimensions (width, height), spacing (margins, padding from parent/siblings), colors (hex, rgba, variable references), typography (font-family, font-size, font-weight, line-height, letter-spacing), and asset export options. Redlines show auto-measured distances: hover between elements to see spacing. Click any layer to see its full properties.

In Dev Mode, component instances show: component name, variant properties, overrides from default, and link to main component. Variables appear as design tokens with their names and resolved values. Annotations: use the annotation tool (in Dev Mode toolbar) to add developer notes pinned to specific design elements. Annotations appear as numbered markers and can include rich text descriptions. Dev Mode supports 'Ready for dev' status markers on sections to indicate handoff-ready designs.

Tool shortcuts: V=Move, F=Frame, R=Rectangle, O=Ellipse, L=Line, T=Text, P=Pen, Shift+P=Pencil, S=Slice, I=Color picker (eyedropper), K=Scale, C=Comment, H=Hand (pan). Quick actions: Ctrl/Cmd+D=Duplicate in place, Alt+drag=Duplicate while moving, Ctrl/Cmd+G=Group, Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G=Ungroup, Shift+A=Add auto layout, Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+K=Create component, Enter=Select child, Escape=Select parent. Hold Space+drag to pan canvas. Scroll to zoom with Ctrl/Cmd+scroll.

Zoom: Ctrl/Cmd+0=Zoom to 100%, Ctrl/Cmd+1=Zoom to fit all, Ctrl/Cmd+2=Zoom to selection, Ctrl/Cmd++=Zoom in, Ctrl/Cmd+-=Zoom out. Properties: Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+C=Copy properties (fill, stroke, effects), Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+V=Paste properties. Layer order: Ctrl/Cmd+]=Bring forward, Ctrl/Cmd+[=Send backward, Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+]=Bring to front, Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+[=Send to back. Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+H=Toggle layer visibility, Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+L=Toggle layer lock. Ctrl/Cmd+R=Rename selected layer.

Fill types: Solid (single color, hex/rgba), Linear gradient (two+ color stops along a line), Radial gradient (circular color spread from center), Angular gradient (colors sweep around a point), Diamond gradient (diamond-shaped spread), Image (bitmap fill with fit, fill, crop, tile modes). Each fill has independent opacity (0-100%). Multiple fills can be stacked on a single element (rendered bottom to top in the fills list). Add fills with the '+' button in the Fill section. Toggle fill visibility with the eye icon.

Blend modes available on fills, strokes, and entire layers: Normal, Darken, Multiply, Color Burn, Lighten, Screen, Color Dodge, Overlay, Soft Light, Hard Light, Difference, Exclusion, Hue, Saturation, Color, Luminosity. Layer opacity (0-100%) affects the entire layer including children. Fill/stroke opacity is independent and affects only that fill or stroke. Use number keys 1-0 for quick opacity: 1=10%, 5=50%, 0=100%, type quickly (e.g., 45=45%). Blend modes are in the fill/stroke color panel dropdown.

Stroke alignment: Inside (stroke drawn inside shape boundary), Outside (stroke drawn outside), Center (stroke centered on boundary). Stroke style: Solid or Dash. Dash customization: set dash length and gap length in the advanced stroke settings. Stroke weight: any positive number, including decimals. Independent stroke weights per side available on frames (top, right, bottom, left). Multiple strokes can be stacked per element. Stroke caps: None, Round, Square (for open paths). Stroke joins: Miter, Bevel, Round.

Drop shadow: renders shadow outside the shape. Properties: X offset (px), Y offset (px), Blur radius (px, 0=hard edge), Spread (px, positive=larger, negative=smaller), Color (with opacity). Inner shadow: renders shadow inside the shape with same properties. Multiple shadows can be stacked (add with '+' button). Toggle shadow visibility with eye icon. Shadow color supports any color with alpha. Typical subtle shadow: x=0, y=2, blur=4, spread=0, color=black at 10-15% opacity. Elevated card: x=0, y=4, blur=16, spread=0.

Layer blur: applies Gaussian blur to the entire layer. Set blur amount in pixels (higher=more blur). Use for: defocused backgrounds, placeholder content, depth effects. Background blur: blurs content BEHIND the layer (the layer itself can be semi-transparent). Use for: glassmorphism, frosted glass UI, overlay panels. Typical glassmorphism: background blur=20-40px with white fill at 20-40% opacity, subtle 1px white border. Multiple effects can be stacked on one layer. Toggle effect visibility with the eye icon per effect.

Text properties: font family, font weight (Thin=100 through Black=900), font size (px), line height (auto, px, or %), letter spacing (px or %), paragraph spacing (px). Text resize behavior: Auto width (single line, grows horizontally), Auto height (fixed width, grows vertically to fit content), Fixed size (fixed width and height, overflows or truncates). Set resize mode in the text section of design panel. Text alignment: Left, Center, Right, Justified. Vertical alignment within text box: Top, Middle, Bottom.

Text decoration: Underline, Strikethrough (set in text panel or via Type > Text Decoration). Text transform: Uppercase, Lowercase, Capitalize, none (via Type > Text Transform or text panel). OpenType features (via Type > OpenType or three-dot menu in text panel): Stylistic sets (ss01-ss20), Standard ligatures, Discretionary ligatures, Contextual alternates, Fractions, Ordinals, Tabular/Proportional figures. List styles: bulleted, numbered. Text as auto layout: text frames can have auto layout applied to create inline layouts with padding and gap.

Layout grids help with alignment and responsive design. Three grid types: Grid (uniform square grid, set size in px), Columns (vertical columns with count, gutter, margin; type=Stretch or Center or Left/Right with fixed width), Rows (horizontal rows with same options). Multiple grids can be stacked on one frame. Toggle grid visibility: Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+4 or click eye icon. Grids are visual guides only and do not affect child positioning. Typical 12-column grid: count=12, gutter=20-32px, margin=24-64px, type=Stretch.

SVG export tips for developers: enable 'Include id attribute' to preserve layer names as HTML ids. Flatten boolean groups before export for cleaner paths. Outline strokes if stroke alignment matters (Object > Outline Stroke). Simplify paths by reducing unnecessary vector points. Use 'Export as SVG' on component instances to get clean SVG. SVG viewBox matches the element dimensions. Remove hidden layers before export to reduce file size. Use the SVG code directly from Dev Mode inspect panel for inline SVG use in code.

Move: arrow keys nudge by 1px, Shift+arrow nudges by 10px (configurable in Preferences > Nudge amount). Resize: drag handles; hold Shift to maintain aspect ratio, hold Alt/Option to resize from center. Enter exact values in W/H fields; use math (e.g., type '200+50' in width field for 250). Constrain proportions with chain icon between W/H. Rotate: hover near corner until rotation cursor appears, drag; hold Shift for 15-degree increments. Set exact rotation angle in the rotation field (0-360 degrees).

Smart animate matches layers by name between source and destination frames and animates changes in position, size, rotation, opacity, and fill. For Smart animate to work: matching layer names must exist in both frames. Use on component variants for micro-interactions: create variants with different states (default, hover, pressed), then add interactions with Smart animate between them. Interactive components: add prototype interactions directly on component variants in the main component set to make all instances automatically interactive.

In Dev Mode, hover over any element to see its dimensions. Hold Alt/Option and hover between elements to see spacing measurements (redlines). Click an element, then hover over another to see the distance between them. Measurements show: absolute position (X, Y from canvas origin), dimensions (W, H), padding (if auto layout), gap (if auto layout), and distances to parent edges. All values displayed in pixels. Use Dev Mode for accurate handoff: developers see the exact same values. Spacing values automatically reference variables/tokens when applied.

Use slash naming for component organization: Icons/Arrow/Right creates nested hierarchy in the assets panel. Layers can be reparented by dragging in the layers panel into different frames. Hold Ctrl/Cmd while dragging to reparent without repositioning. Collapsed frames in the layers panel: click arrow to expand/collapse. Search layers: use Ctrl/Cmd+F in the layers panel to find layers by name. Layer colors: right-click a layer to assign a color tag (red, orange, yellow, green, blue, violet, gray) for visual organization.
